evidence: The paper explicitly states: "The variables were ranked according to their permutation variable importance" in the Random Survival Forest analysis using the ranger package. This is not impurity-based (Gini/MDI) importance. The paper ranks biomarkers by permutation importance (Figure 1), identifying cTnT-hs as "the strongest association" followed by NT-proBNP. It uses Boruta algorithm for feature selection (permutation-based) and validates findings with adjusted Cox regression in an independent validation cohort (RE-LY), providing robust corroboration.
